records.ety: [L. omen, the original form being osmen, according to Varro.]

defs: An occurrence supposed to portend, or show the character of, some future event; any indication or action regarded as a foreshowing; a foreboding; a presage; an augury.
quotes: Bid go with evil omen, and the brand Of infamy upon my name.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Omened (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Omening.]

defs: To divine or to foreshow by signs or portents; to have omens or premonitions regarding; to predict; to augur; as, to omen ill of an enterprise.
quotes: The yet unknown verdict, of which, however, all omened the tragical contents.
